title: Government Fees, Administrative Costs and Taxes; Establishment and Administration of the Congressional Migrant Workers Scholarship Fund
content:
Sec. 4. Government Fees, Administrative Costs and Taxes.

All fees for services charged by any government agency on migrant workers prevailing at the time of the effectivity of this Rule shall not be increased. All other services rendered by the DOLE and other government agencies in connection with the recruitment, placement, and assistance to migrant workers shall be rendered free. The administrative cost thereof shall not be borne by the worker.

Migrant workers are exempt from payment of travel tax and airport fee upon proper showing of the Overseas Employment Certificate (OEC) issued by the POEA.

Remittances of all OFWs, upon showing of the OEC or valid OWWA Membership Certificate, shall be exempt from documentary stamp tax (DST) under Section 181 of the National Internal Revenue Code, as amended.

A duplicate or certified true copy of the valid proof of entitlement shall be secured by the OFW from POEA or OWWA for use by beneficiaries to avail DST exemption.

OFWs sending remittances through banks must show valid proof of entitlement when arranging remittance transfers. Invalid proofs do not entitle DST exemption.

The Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) may promulgate regulations for effective implementation of these exemptions.

Sec. 5. Establishment of the Congressional Migrant Workers Scholarship Fund.

A fund of One Hundred Fifty Million Pesos (P150,000,000.00), sourced from Lotto proceeds, is created to benefit deserving migrant workers and/or their immediate descendants pursuing science and technology courses or training as defined by DOST.

Sec. 6. Creation of the Scholarship Fund Committee.

A Scholarship Fund Committee is created, composed of representatives from DOLE, DOST, POEA, OWWA, TESDA, and two migrant worker representatives appointed by the Secretary of Labor and Employment.

Sec. 7. Functions of the Scholarship Fund Committee.

(a) Set coverage, criteria, and admission standards for the Scholarship Program;
(b) Determine amount of availment;
(c) Monitor and evaluate the program;
(d) Identify/accredit training and testing institutions;
(e) Perform other functions necessary to attain the Fund's purpose.

Sec. 8. Implementing Agency.

OWWA shall serve as Secretariat of the Scholarship Fund Committee and administer the Scholarship Program in coordination with DOST.
